title: LivAna
subtitle: Full-Stack Student Services Platform
problem: Students typically juggle disconnected apps for PG stays, mess plans, and grocery needs, which creates fragmented service and poor operational visibility.
approach: Spearheading data modeling and backend architecture for a multi-service platform. Designed a 14-table PostgreSQL schema and optimized read-heavy query patterns using targeted denormalization. Architected 58 Spring Boot REST API endpoints for low-latency, role-based data serving.
outcomes: 58 REST API endpoints in Spring Boot
14-table PostgreSQL schema with optimized read patterns
Low-latency role-based access for frontend consumption
Covers PG accommodations, mess meal plans, and grocery services

title: AI-Driven Dynamic Credit Limit Optimization
subtitle: AI-Driven Dynamic Credit Limit Optimization
problem: Static credit policy rules react too slowly to behavior shifts and fail to optimize risk-adjusted portfolio performance in real time.
approach: Engineered a high-throughput FastAPI backend handling 5,000+ events per minute with sub-second latency. Integrated a Double DQN PyTorch decision model directly into the event stream for real-time credit limit decisions under portfolio constraints.
outcomes: Sub-second decision latency under streaming load
Real-time policy updates using Double DQN

title: DOC-OC
subtitle: Automated Marksheet Extraction System
problem: Academic marksheets arrive in inconsistent formats, making manual extraction slow, unreliable, and difficult to scale in internship workflows.
approach: Developed a custom parsing pipeline to ingest and extract structured information from multi-format documents using YOLO and Table Transformers. Built an async FastAPI backend to serve real-time extraction data, and deployed the system in production during internship.
outcomes: Structured extraction from multi-format academic documents
Async FastAPI backend for real-time serving

title: Cargo Laytime
subtitle: Maritime SOF Document Processing
problem: Unstructured maritime SOF documents require heavy manual effort to convert into reliable event timelines for operations and laytime analysis.
approach: Developed a custom parsing system to transform raw maritime SOF documents into clean, structured relational event data with backend orchestration and production-minded validation.
